prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `5623160b`.
codex_response:
The task rule is: keep the connected `9` framework fixed, and slide each other connected object straight away from the side where it touches the `9`s until that object hits the matching border, preserving shape and orientation. I discarded the sonnet hint because it incorrectly described a 180-degree rotation; I kept the opus hint, and documented that correction in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5623160b/notes.md#L1).

The package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5623160b/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5623160b/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_5623160b/generator.py#L178). The verifier uses DSL composition to detect which side of the `9` hub each object touches and compute its outward border shift; the generator builds connected `9` frameworks plus attached colored motifs across at least three directions, which matches the official family. No new reusable primitive was needed.

Validation passed end-to-end: `4/4` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, and the artifact set exists at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/previews/preview_03.png). I also opened [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/5623160b/previews/preview_01.png) and it looks on-family.
